briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, raising serious questions about consumer protections in fintech.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a homegrown sovereign payment network, signalling a major shift away from US-dominated card networks.

- **[XTransfer and Societe Generale Partner To Streamline Cross-Border Payments for Global Trade Transactions](https://financialpost.com/globe-newswire/xtransfer-and-societe-generale-partner-to-streamline-cross-border-payments-for-global-trade-transactions)** — *Financial Post* — B2B cross-border payment platform XTransfer has partnered with Societe Generale to simplify international trade payments for businesses worldwide.

- **[Stablecoins will eventually "leapfrog" real-time rail payments, finance leaders say](https://betakit.com/stablecoins-will-eventually-leapfrog-real-time-rail-payments-finance-leaders-say/)** — *BetaKit* — Senior finance leaders predict stablecoins will ultimately surpass traditional real-time payment rails as the dominant infrastructure for digital transactions.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Stablecoins will eventually "leapfrog" real-time rail payments, finance leaders say](https://betakit.com/stablecoins-will-eventually-leapfrog-real-time-rail-payments-finance-leaders-say/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian finance leaders weigh in on how stablecoins could displace real-time payment rails, with significant implications for Canada's payments modernization agenda.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada officially labelled screen scraping as insecure and committed to an open banking framework, laying the groundwork for the consumer-driven banking legislation now in progress.

- **[Shopify ups share buyback program as activist investors question AI policies](https://betakit.com/shopify-ups-share-buyback-program-as-activist-investors-question-ai-policies/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian e-commerce and payments giant Shopify has expanded its share buyback program amid growing pressure from activist investors over its artificial intelligence strategy.

- **[SR&ED season is here. How to claim the hours you actually worked](https://betakit.com/sred-season-is-here-how-to-claim-the-hours-you-actually-worked/)** — *BetaKit* — A timely guide for Canadian tech and fintech startups on accurately claiming labour hours under the federal SR&ED tax incentive program.

- **[Carney addresses technical recession, says economy going through 'settling-in' period](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/carney-responds-to-recessison-news-9.7220490?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Prime Minister Mark Carney acknowledged Canada's technical recession, framing it as a transitional phase — a macroeconomic backdrop with direct implications for fintech lending, credit, and investment activity.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis of how open banking frameworks reshape payments competition, directly relevant to Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintechs preparing for Canada's consumer-driven banking rollout.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit card products into new and unconventional territory, offering lessons for Atlantic Canadian startups and credit unions exploring embedded finance and alternative credit products.

- **[Supersized data centres are coming to Canada. One province is at the epicentre](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bakx-york-ai-data-centres-alberta-solomon-9.7222388?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's data centre boom driven by AI demand raises important questions about digital infrastructure investment that Atlantic Canada could leverage to attract fintech and tech sector growth.
